summary: First backend test iteration for La Clase Digital. 22/23 pytest tests passed. Auth (magic-link), course content protection, dashboard, admin endpoints, submissions/feedback, forum threads, founder-pricing logic and admin seeding all verified end-to-end against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. One backend bug found: GET /api/checkout/status/{session_id} returns 500 instead of a graceful response when the underlying Stripe call fails.

critical.issue: Returns HTTP 500 when emergentintegrations raises CheckoutError (`No such checkout.session`). The session_id was just created moments earlier by /api/checkout/create with the same sk_test_emergent key, so a freshly-created session cannot be polled. The route should try/except the Stripe call and return a soft response (e.g. payment_status='unknown' or 502 with detail) so the success page does not crash. Reproduced consistently. Backend log: `emergentintegrations.payments.stripe.checkout.CheckoutError: Failed to retrieve session status: ... No such checkout.session: cs_test_...`

minor.issue: Resend returns 403: 'The laclasedigital.com domain is not verified'. send_email already swallows the error so the API still returns 200, but no email is actually delivered. Domain must be verified in Resend dashboard for production.

action_items: Wrap `stripe.get_checkout_status(session_id)` in /api/checkout/status/{session_id} in a try/except to avoid 500 when the Stripe key cannot retrieve the freshly-created session. Recommended: return {status:'unknown', payment_status:'unknown', enrollment:None} or HTTP 502 with `detail` so the success page stays functional.
Verify the laclasedigital.com domain in Resend so magic-link and notification emails are actually delivered (currently 403 from Resend → send_email is a no-op).
Consider replacing sk_test_emergent with a real Stripe test key for end-to-end paid-flow validation; currently checkout/create works but status polling and webhook completion cannot be exercised.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~950 lines — split into routers/services/models for maintainability (auth, courses, admin, payments).
/api/checkout/status has no error handling around the third-party call; any Stripe failure propagates as 500.
Datetime serialization in clean_doc only walks the top level — nested datetimes (none today, but risk for future fields) would not be converted.

Magic-link endpoint /api/auth/request-link returns {ok:true} even for non-existing users (good for enumeration safety) but also when Resend fails — consider returning a distinct flag in non-prod for debuggability.
Admin seed uses ADMIN_EMAIL.lower() consistently — good. However the admin id is derived from MD5 of the *unlowered* email; if ADMIN_EMAIL casing changes the admin user could end up with two ids.
test_credentials: Admin: benitezl@go.ugr.es (auto-seeded role=admin). JWTs generated programmatically in tests via MAGIC_LINK_SECRET → /api/auth/verify, no email needed.
seed_data_creation: Created ephemeral test users (TEST_*@example.com, test.student+<ts>@example.com) and one direct-DB enrollment for the test student to exercise course content endpoints. Submission, threads and admin feedback rows also created. All data is non-destructive but persists in MongoDB; cleanup not implemented (would require a /api/admin/cleanup helper).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/. Run with: `cd /app && python -m pytest backend/tests/ -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. The conftest auto-loads /app/backend/.env, generates magic tokens with MAGIC_LINK_SECRET and verifies them via /api/auth/verify to obtain real JWTs (no email required). Admin role is granted automatically because the verify route checks against ADMIN_EMAIL. The student-enrollment fixture writes directly to MongoDB (motor) to bypass Stripe. Only failing test: TestCheckout::test_checkout_status (waits on a 500 fix in /api/checkout/status).
rca of the issue: RCA for /api/checkout/status 500: emergentintegrations.payments.stripe.checkout.StripeCheckout.get_checkout_status raises CheckoutError when Stripe responds with 'No such checkout.session'. The route handler in server.py calls it without try/except, so FastAPI converts the unhandled exception into a 500. Reproduction: 1) POST /api/checkout/create {course_slug:'ia-ele', origin_url:'https://teach-preview.preview.emergentagent.com'} → 200 returns session_id. 2) Immediately GET /api/checkout/status/<that session_id> → 500 with body 'Internal Server Error'. Backend log shows the CheckoutError. Mitigation: wrap the call in try/except CheckoutError and return a soft response (e.g. {status:'unknown', payment_status:'unknown', enrollment:None, error:'stripe_unavailable'}) and log a warning. Long-term: configure a working Stripe test key.
